answer:
**Clay Shaw was a former Director of the International Trade Mart in New Orleans who was investigated by New Orleans District Attorney James C. Garrison in connection with the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.**
- Clay Shaw's name did not come up during the initial FBI investigation of the assassination, and the FBI never investigated him [1].
- Shaw was arrested by Garrison, who claimed Shaw was identical to a Clay Bertrand, an alleged contact of New Orleans attorney Dean Andrews [1].
- The CIA had a relationship with Shaw, and he had a security clearance, but the details of this relationship are not fully explained in the documents [2].
- Shaw was also mentioned in a Pravda article, which claimed he was involved with the CIA in Italy, but this claim was largely disputed [2].
* *_The records do not provide a comprehensive biography of Clay Shaw or fully explain the nature of his relationship with the CIA._*
